questions.question: Why is improvement in food resources necessary?
questions.answer: It is necessary to meet the food demand of the growing population.

questions.question: Name two activities included in animal husbandry.
questions.answer: Feeding and breeding of livestock.

questions.question: Give two examples of cereal crops.
questions.answer: Wheat and rice.

questions.question: What is crop variety improvement?
questions.answer: It is the development of crop varieties with desirable characteristics such as higher yield and disease resistance.

questions.question: Name two desirable characteristics of improved crop varieties.
questions.answer: High yield and resistance to diseases.

questions.question: What is nutrient management?
questions.answer: It is the practice of supplying essential nutrients to crops through manure and fertilizers.

questions.question: Name two macronutrients required by plants.
questions.answer: Nitrogen and phosphorus.

questions.question: What is vermicompost?
questions.answer: Vermicompost is compost prepared using earthworms.

questions.question: What is green manure?
questions.answer: Green manure is prepared by ploughing green plants into the soil to enrich it with nutrients.

questions.question: What is crop rotation?
questions.answer: Crop rotation is the growing of different crops in succession on the same land.

questions.question: What is intercropping?
questions.answer: Intercropping is growing two or more crops together in a definite row pattern.

questions.question: Why is irrigation important for crops?
questions.answer: Irrigation supplies water required for crop growth.

questions.question: Name two sources of irrigation water.
questions.answer: Wells and canals.

questions.question: Why are weeds harmful to crops?
questions.answer: They compete with crops for nutrients, water and sunlight.

questions.question: What are herbicides?
questions.answer: Herbicides are chemicals used to kill weeds.

questions.question: What is the main aim of poultry farming?
questions.answer: To produce eggs and meat.

questions.question: What are broilers?
questions.answer: Broilers are poultry birds raised for meat production.

questions.question: What are layers in poultry farming?
questions.answer: Layers are poultry birds raised for egg production.

questions.question: What is capture fishing?
questions.answer: Capture fishing is catching fish from natural water bodies.

questions.question: What is mariculture?
questions.answer: Mariculture is the culture of marine fish in seawater.

questions.question: What is aquaculture?
questions.answer: Aquaculture is the farming of fish and other aquatic organisms.

questions.question: Why is composite fish culture practiced?
questions.answer: To increase fish production by using different food resources in the pond.

questions.question: Name one exotic breed of cattle.
questions.answer: Jersey.

questions.question: What is apiculture?
questions.answer: Apiculture is the rearing of bees for honey production.

questions.question: Name one product obtained from bee-keeping.
questions.answer: Honey.
